title: Rubus obcordatus
taxonomy: Rubus obcordatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is classified within the genus Rubus, which includes raspberries, blackberries, and thimbleberries. The genus Rubus is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as brambles or rubus species. Rubus obcordatus is native to the southeastern United States and is a shrub that typically grows in wetland environments. The plant is often associated with other bramble species due to its similar growth habits and fruit characteristics.

intro: Rubus obcordatus, commonly known as the heartleaf dewberry, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. The heartleaf dewberry has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rubus obcordatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
